{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_4c7bbc4050274ca3ac8bb176","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Pluck Debug Flags and Logging Configuration\n2\t\n3\t**Bead:** bf-5p3g  \n4\t**Component:** NEEDLE Pluck Strand  \n5\t**Source:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs`  \n6\t**Date:** 2026-07-09\n7\t\n8\t## Overview\n9\t\n10\tPluck is the primary bead selection strand in NEEDLE, handling >90% of all bead processing. It uses the Rust `tracing` crate for structured logging with multiple log levels. Debug logging is controlled via the `RUST_LOG` environment variable.\n11\t\n12\t## Available Debug Flags\n13\t\n14\t### Primary Environment Variable: `RUST_LOG`\n15\t\n16\tThe `RUST_LOG` environment variable controls which log statements are emitted. The `tracing-subscriber` crate's `env-filter` feature parses this variable at runtime.\n17\t\n18\t#### Syntax\n19\t```\n20\tRUST_LOG=<target>=<level>,<target2>=<level2>,...\n21\t```\n22\t\n23\t#### Available Log Levels\n24\t- `error` - Failures that prevent operation\n25\t- `warn` - (None currently used in Pluck)\n26\t- `info` - Significant events (split trigger, candidate return)\n27\t- `debug` - Detailed operation trace (filter stages, counts, decisions)\n28\t- `trace` - Most granular details (reserved for future expansion)\n29\t\n30\t#### Target Paths for Pluck\n31\t- `needle::strand::pluck` - Pluck strand specific\n32\t- `needle::strand` - All strand modules\n33\t- `needle::bead_store` - Bead store operations\n34\t- `needle::worker` - Worker lifecycle\n35\t- `needle::dispatch` - Agent dispatch\n36\t- `needle` - All NEEDLE modules\n37\t- (no target) - All crates globally\n38\t\n39\t### Common Usage Patterns\n40\t\n41\t#### Pluck-only Debug\n42\t```bash\n43\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n44\t```\n45\t\n46\t#### Pluck-only Trace (Maximum Verbosity)\n47\t```bash\n48\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n49\t```\n50\t\n51\t#### All NEEDLE Debug\n52\t```bash\n53\tRUST_LOG=needle=debug\n54\t```\n55\t\n56\t#### Everything Debug (Very Verbose)\n57\t```bash\n58\tRUST_LOG=debug\n59\t```\n60\t\n61\t#### Comprehensive Pluck Capture (Recommended)\n62\t```bash\n63\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug\n64\t```\n65\t\n66\t## Pluck Debug Events\n67\t\n68\t### 1. Evaluation Start\n69\t```\n70\tDEBUG Pluck strand evaluation starting\n71\t  exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n72\t  split_threshold: 3\n73\t```\n74\t\n75\t### 2. Bead Store Query\n76\t```\n77\tDEBUG Querying bead store for ready candidates\n78\t  filters: Filters { assignee: None, exclude_labels: [...] }\n79\t```\n80\t\n81\t### 3. Store Results\n82\t```\n83\tDEBUG Bead store returned {count} candidates\n84\t  count: 42\n85\t```\n86\t\n87\t### 4. Label Filtering\n88\tWhen beads are excluded:\n89\t```\n90\tDEBUG Label filtering excluded {excluded_count} beads\n91\t  excluded_count: 5\n92\t  remaining: 37\n93\t  excluded_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n94\t```\n95\t\n96\tFor each excluded bead:\n97\t```\n98\tDEBUG Excluded bead due to labels\n99\t  bead_id: \"bf-1234\"\n100\t  labels: [\"deferred\", \"priority:p2\"]\n101\t  excluded_reasons: [\"deferred\"]\n102\t```\n103\t\n104\t### 5. Status/Assignee Filtering\n105\t```\n106\tDEBUG Status/assignee filtering removed {filtered_count} beads\n107\t  filtered_count: 2\n108\t  remaining: 35\n109\t```\n110\t\n111\t### 6. Sorting\n112\t```\n113\tDEBUG Sorting {total} candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\n114\t  total: 35\n115\t  first_bead_id: \"bf-0001\"\n116\t  first_priority: 1\n117\t  first_created_at: \"2026-07-09T00:00:00Z\"\n118\t```\n119\t\n120\t### 7. Split Trigger Check\n121\t```\n122\tDEBUG Checking split trigger for first candidate\n123\t  bead_id: \"bf-0001\"\n124\t  failure_count: 0\n125\t  threshold: 3\n126\t  split_triggered: false\n127\t```\n128\t\n129\tWhen split is triggered:\n130\t```\n131\tINFO Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\n132\t  bead_id: \"bf-0001\"\n133\t  failure_count: 4\n134\t  threshold: 3\n135\t```\n136\t\n137\t### 8. Result Return\n138\tWhen candidates are found:\n139\t```\n140\tINFO Returning {count} candidates for processing\n141\t  count: 35\n142\t  candidates: [\"bf-0001\", \"bf-0002\", \"bf-0003\", ...]\n143\t```\n144\t\n145\tWhen no candidates remain:\n146\t```\n147\tDEBUG No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\n148\t```\n149\t\n150\t### 9. Error Cases\n151\t```\n152\tERROR Bead store query failed\n153\t  error: <error details>\n154\t```\n155\t\n156\t## How to Enable Debug Output\n157\t\n158\t### Method 1: Direct Environment Variable\n159\t```bash\n160\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n161\tneedle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR\n162\t```\n163\t\n164\t### Method 2: Inline with Command\n165\t```bash\n166\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR\n167\t```\n168\t\n169\t### Method 3: Capture to File\n170\t```bash\n171\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n172\t```\n173\t\n174\t### Method 4: Comprehensive Capture Script\n175\tThe provided `capture-pluck-debug.sh` script demonstrates a complete capture:\n176\t```bash\n177\t./capture-pluck-debug.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug-output.log 1\n178\t```\n179\t\n180\tThis sets:\n181\t```bash\n182\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug\n183\t```\n184\t\n185\t## Filtering Decision Logging\n186\t\n187\t### Which Events Show Filtering Decisions?\n188\t\n189\tAll filter stages emit structured events:\n190\t\n191\t1. **Label filtering**: Logs count of excluded beads and reasons\n192\t2. **Status/assignee filtering**: Logs count of removed beads\n193\t3. **Individual exclusions**: Each excluded bead logs its ID, labels, and exclusion reasons\n194\t\n195\t### Key Fields for Debugging Filtering\n196\t\n197\t- `exclude_labels` - Labels being filtered\n198\t- `excluded_count` - How many beads were excluded\n199\t- `remaining` - How many beads remain after filtering\n200\t- `excluded_reasons` - Which labels caused exclusion\n201\t- `filters` - The filter object passed to the bead store\n202\t\n203\t## CLI Flags\n204\t\n205\t**Note:** NEEDLE does not provide CLI flags for debug logging configuration. All logging is controlled via the `RUST_LOG` environment variable.\n206\t\n207\tHowever, NEEDLE provides related logging commands:\n208\t\n209\t### `needle logs`\n210\tView and query telemetry logs:\n211\t```bash\n212\tneedle logs --follow                    # Stream events in real-time\n213\tneedle logs --filter 'event_type=bead.*'  # Filter events\n214\tneedle logs --since 1h                   # Events since 1 hour ago\n215\tneedle logs --format json               # JSON Lines output\n216\t```\n217\t\n218\t### `needle config --dump`\n219\tView resolved configuration including logging setup:\n220\t```bash\n221\tneedle config --dump                    # Show all config\n222\tneedle config --get telemetry.file_sink.enabled  # Get specific value\n223\t```\n224\t\n225\t## Tracing Instrumentation Details\n226\t\n227\t### Span Fields\n228\tAll Pluck events include these fields:\n229\t- `strand`: \"pluck\" - identifies the strand type\n230\t- `exclude_labels`: List of labels being filtered\n231\t- `split_threshold`: Failure count threshold for auto-split\n232\t\n233\t### Event Types\n234\t- `tracing::debug!()` - Detailed diagnostic information\n235\t- `tracing::info!()` - Significant operational events\n236\t- `tracing::error!()` - Failure events\n237\t\n238\t### Span Name\n239\tAll Pluck operations are tracked in the `strand.pluck` span.\n240\t\n241\t## Configuration Sources\n242\t\n243\tThe tracing subscriber is initialized in `src/cli/mod.rs`:\n244\t1. It uses the Rust `tracing` crate for structured logging with multiple log levels. Debug logging is controlled via the `RUST_LOG` environment variable.\n\n## Available Debug Flags\n\n### Primary Environment Variable: `RUST_LOG`\n\nThe `RUST_LOG` environment variable controls which log statements are emitted. The `tracing-subscriber` crate's `env-filter` feature parses this variable at runtime.\n\n#### Syntax\n```\nRUST_LOG=<target>=<level>,<target2>=<level2>,...\n```\n\n#### Available Log Levels\n- `error` - Failures that prevent operation\n- `warn` - (None currently used in Pluck)\n- `info` - Significant events (split trigger, candidate return)\n- `debug` - Detailed operation trace (filter stages, counts, decisions)\n- `trace` - Most granular details (reserved for future expansion)\n\n#### Target Paths for Pluck\n- `needle::strand::pluck` - Pluck strand specific\n- `needle::strand` - All strand modules\n- `needle::bead_store` - Bead store operations\n- `needle::worker` - Worker lifecycle\n- `needle::dispatch` - Agent dispatch\n- `needle` - All NEEDLE modules\n- (no target) - All crates globally\n\n### Common Usage Patterns\n\n#### Pluck-only Debug\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n```\n\n#### Pluck-only Trace (Maximum Verbosity)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n```\n\n#### All NEEDLE Debug\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle=debug\n```\n\n#### Everything Debug (Very Verbose)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=debug\n```\n\n#### Comprehensive Pluck Capture (Recommended)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug\n```\n\n## Pluck Debug Events\n\n### 1. Evaluation Start\n```\nDEBUG Pluck strand evaluation starting\n  exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n  split_threshold: 3\n```\n\n### 2. Bead Store Query\n```\nDEBUG Querying bead store for ready candidates\n  filters: Filters { assignee: None, exclude_labels: [...] }\n```\n\n### 3. Store Results\n```\nDEBUG Bead store returned {count} candidates\n  count: 42\n```\n\n### 4. Label Filtering\nWhen beads are excluded:\n```\nDEBUG Label filtering excluded {excluded_count} beads\n  excluded_count: 5\n  remaining: 37\n  excluded_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n```\n\nFor each excluded bead:\n```\nDEBUG Excluded bead due to labels\n  bead_id: \"bf-1234\"\n  labels: [\"deferred\", \"priority:p2\"]\n  excluded_reasons: [\"deferred\"]\n```\n\n### 5. Status/Assignee Filtering\n```\nDEBUG Status/assignee filtering removed {filtered_count} beads\n  filtered_count: 2\n  remaining: 35\n```\n\n### 6. Sorting\n```\nDEBUG Sorting {total} candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\n  total: 35\n  first_bead_id: \"bf-0001\"\n  first_priority: 1\n  first_created_at: \"2026-07-09T00:00:00Z\"\n```\n\n### 7. Split Trigger Check\n```\nDEBUG Checking split trigger for first candidate\n  bead_id: \"bf-0001\"\n  failure_count: 0\n  threshold: 3\n  split_triggered: false\n```\n\nWhen split is triggered:\n```\nINFO Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\n  bead_id: \"bf-0001\"\n  failure_count: 4\n  threshold: 3\n```\n\n### 8. Result Return\nWhen candidates are found:\n```\nINFO Returning {count} candidates for processing\n  count: 35\n  candidates: [\"bf-0001\", \"bf-0002\", \"bf-0003\", ...]\n```\n\nWhen no candidates remain:\n```\nDEBUG No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\n```\n\n### 9. Error Cases\n```\nERROR Bead store query failed\n  error: <error details>\n```\n\n## How to Enable Debug Output\n\n### Method 1: Direct Environment Variable\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\nneedle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR\n```\n\n### Method 2: Inline with Command\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR\n```\n\n### Method 3: Capture to File\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n### Method 4: Comprehensive Capture Script\nThe provided `capture-pluck-debug.sh` script demonstrates a complete capture:\n```bash\n./capture-pluck-debug.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug-output.log 1\n```\n\nThis sets:\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug\n```\n\n## Filtering Decision Logging\n\n### Which Events Show Filtering Decisions?\n\nAll filter stages emit structured events:\n\n1. **Label filtering**: Logs count of excluded beads and reasons\n2. **Status/assignee filtering**: Logs count of removed beads\n3. **Individual exclusions**: Each excluded bead logs its ID, labels, and exclusion reasons\n\n### Key Fields for Debugging Filtering\n\n- `exclude_labels` - Labels being filtered\n- `excluded_count` - How many beads were excluded\n- `remaining` - How many beads remain after filtering\n- `excluded_reasons` - Which labels caused exclusion\n- `filters` - The filter object passed to the bead store\n\n## CLI Flags\n\n**Note:** NEEDLE does not provide CLI flags for debug logging configuration. All logging is controlled via the `RUST_LOG` environment variable.\n\nHowever, NEEDLE provides related logging commands:\n\n### `needle logs`\nView and query telemetry logs:\n```bash\nneedle logs --follow                    # Stream events in real-time\nneedle logs --filter 'event_type=bead.*'  # Filter events\nneedle logs --since 1h                   # Events since 1 hour ago\nneedle logs --format json               # JSON Lines output\n```\n\n### `needle config --dump`\nView resolved configuration including logging setup:\n```bash\nneedle config --dump                    # Show all config\nneedle config --get telemetry.file_sink.enabled  # Get specific value\n```\n\n## Tracing Instrumentation Details\n\n### Span Fields\nAll Pluck events include these fields:\n- `strand`: \"pluck\" - identifies the strand type\n- `exclude_labels`: List of labels being filtered\n- `split_threshold`: Failure count threshold for auto-split\n\n### Event Types\n- `tracing::debug!()` - Detailed diagnostic information\n- `tracing::info!()` - Significant operational events\n- `tracing::error!()` - Failure events\n\n### Span Name\nAll Pluck operations are tracked in the `strand.pluck` span.\n\n## Configuration Sources\n\nThe tracing subscriber is initialized in `src/cli/mod.rs`:\n1. OTLP layer (if enabled in config)\n2. fmt layer for stderr output\n3. Environment variable filter (via `tracing-subscriber`'s `env-filter` feature)\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Verification\n\n✅ **List of available debug flags/variables found**\n- Primary: `RUST_LOG` environment variable\n- Levels: error, warn, info, debug, trace\n- Targets: needle::strand::pluck, needle::strand, needle, etc.\n\n✅ **Documentation of which flags control filtering decision logging**\n- `RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug` enables all filtering decision logs\n- Individual bead exclusions logged at DEBUG level\n- Filter stage counts logged at DEBUG level\n- Split trigger decisions logged at DEBUG/INFO level\n\n✅ **Clear instructions on how to enable debug output**\n- Multiple methods documented (env var, inline, file capture, script)\n- Comprehensive capture script provided\n- Examples for all common scenarios\n\n## Related Documentation\n\n- **Pluck Filter Configurations:** See `bf-1jwl.md` for complete filter documentation\n- **Pluck Debug Logging Guide:** See `bf-2hvf.md` for detailed event examples\n- **Pluck exclude_labels:** See `bf-ogec.md` for exclude_labels extraction\n- **NEEDLE Logging:** Check NEEDLE project docs for global logging configuration\n- **capture-pluck-debug.sh:** Shell script demonstrating comprehensive debug capture\n\n## Future Enhancements\n\nPossible improvements for future work:\n\n1. **CLI Flags:** Add `--log-level` flag to avoid environment variable manipulation\n2. **Filter Configuration:** Add ability to enable/disable specific log categories\n3. **Structured JSON Output:** Emit logs in JSON format for easier parsing\n4. **Per-Bead Timings:** Add timing information for each filtering stage\n5. **Filter Decision Logs:** Emit specific reason for each bead's inclusion/exclusion\n\n---\n\n**Status:** ✅ Complete - Pluck debug flags and logging configuration documented\n","numLines":287,"startLine":1,"totalLines":287}}}

exclude_labels\n55\t        };\n56\t        PluckStrand {\n57\t            exclude_labels: labels,\n58\t            split_after_failures,\n59\t        }\n60\t    }\n61\t\n62\t    /// Extract the failure count from a bead's labels.\n63\t    ///\n64\t    /// Labels follow the pattern `failure-count:N`. Returns the count if found,\n65\t    /// or 0 if no failure-count label exists.\n66\t    fn extract_failure_count(bead: &Bead) -> u32 {\n67\t        bead.labels\n68\t            .iter()\n69\t            .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n70\t            .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())\n71\t            .max()\n72\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n73\t    }\n74\t\n75\t    /// Sort candidates in deterministic priority order.\n76\t    ///\n77\t    /// Sort key: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n78\t    /// The id tie-breaker ensures identical ordering across platforms.\n79\t    fn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n80\t        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n81\t            a.priority\n82\t                .cmp(&b.priority)\n83\t                .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n84\t                .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n85\t        });\n86\t    }\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\t#[async_trait::async_trait]\n90\timpl super::Strand for PluckStrand {\n91\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n92\t        \"pluck\"\n93\t    }\n94\t\n95\t    #[tracing::instrument(\n96\t        name = \"strand.pluck\",\n97\t        skip(self, store),\n98\t        fields(\n99\t            strand = \"pluck\",\n100\t            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n101\t            split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n102\t        )\n103\t    )]\n104\t    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n105\t        tracing::debug!(\n106\t            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n107\t            split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n108\t            \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\"\n109\t        );\n110\t\n111\t        // 1. Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n112\t        let filters = Filters {\n113\t            assignee: None,\n114\t            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n115\t        };\n116\t\n117\t        tracing::debug!(\n118\t            filters = ?filters,\n119\t            \"Querying bead store for ready candidates\"\n120\t        );\n121\t\n122\t        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n123\t            Ok(beads) => {\n124\t                tracing::debug!(\n125\t                    count = beads.len(),\n126\t                    \"Bead store returned {} candidates\",\n127\t                    beads.len()\n128\t                );\n129\t                beads\n130\t            }\n131\t            Err(e) => {\n132\t                tracing::error!(\n133\t                    error = %e,\n134\t                    \"Bead store query failed\"\n135\t                );\n136\t                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n137\t                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n138\t            }\n139\t        };\n140\t\n141\t        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n142\t        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n143\t        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n144\t        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n145\t        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n146\t        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n147\t        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n148\t        let before_label_filter = candidates.len();\n149\t        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n150\t        let after_label_filter = candidates.len();\n151\t\n152\t        if before_label_filter != after_label_filter {\n153\t            tracing::debug!(\n154\t                excluded_count = before_label_filter - after_label_filter,\n155\t                remaining = after_label_filter,\n156\t                excluded_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n157\t                \"Label filtering excluded {} beads\",\n158\t                before_label_filter - after_label_filter\n159\t            );\n160\t\n161\t            // Log each excluded bead at DEBUG level\n162\t            let excluded_beads: Vec<_> = candidates.iter()\n163\t                .filter(|b| b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)))\n164\t                .map(|b| (b.id.as_ref(), b.labels.clone()))\n165\t                .collect();\n166\t            for (id, labels) in excluded_beads {\n167\t                let excluded_reasons: Vec<_> = labels.iter()\n168\t                    .filter(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l))\n169\t                    .map(|l| l.as_str())\n170\t                    .collect();\n171\t                tracing::debug!(\n172\t                    bead_id = %id,\n173\t                    labels = ?labels,\n174\t                    excluded_reasons = ?excluded_reasons,\n175\t                    \"Excluded bead due to labels\"\n176\t                );\n177\t            }\n178\t\n179\t            // Re-filter since we logged them\n180\t            candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n181\t        } else {\n182\t            tracing::debug!(\n183\t                count = after_label_filter,\n184\t                \"No beads excluded by label filter\"\n185\t            );\n186\t        }\n187\t\n188\t        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker)\n189\t        //    and Open beads with a stale assignee. These are never claimable — the claimer\n190\t        //    will reject them every time, causing a hot loop.\n191\t        let before_status_filter = candidates.len();\n192\t        candidates.retain(|b| {\n193\t            !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n194\t                || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n195\t        });\n196\t        let after_status_filter = candidates.len();\n197\t\n198\t        if before_status_filter != after_status_filter {\n199\t            tracing::debug!(\n200\t                filtered_count = before_status_filter - after_status_filter,\n201\t                remaining = after_status_filter,\n202\t                \"Status/assignee filtering removed {} beads\",\n203\t                before_status_filter - after_status_filter\n204\t            );\n205\t        } else {\n206\t            tracing::debug!(\n207\t                count = after_status_filter,\n208\t                \"No beads excluded by status/assignee filter\"\n209\t            );\n210\t        }\n211\t\n212\t        // 4. Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n213\t        if !candidates.is_empty() {\n214\t            let first = &candidates[0];\n215\t            tracing::debug!(\n216\t                total = candidates.len(),\n217\t                first_bead_id = %first.id,\n218\t                first_priority = first.priority,\n219\t                first_created_at = %first.created_at,\n220\t                \"Sorting {} candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\",\n221\t                candidates.len()\n222\t            );\n223\t        }\n224\t        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n225\t\n226\t        // 5. Check for split trigger: if the first candidate has accumulated\n227\t        //    enough consecutive failures, dispatch a SPLIT instruction instead\n228\t        //    of returning the bead for normal processing.\n229\t        if self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n230\t            if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n231\t                let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n232\t                tracing::debug!(\n233\t                    bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n234\t                    failure_count = failure_count,\n235\t                    threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n236\t                    split_triggered = failure_count >= self.split_after_failures,\n237\t                    \"Checking split trigger for first candidate\"\n238\t                );\n239\t\n240\t                if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n241\t                    tracing::info!(\n242\t                        bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n243\t                        failure_count = failure_count,\n244\t                        threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n245\t                        \"Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\"\n246\t                    );\n247\t                    return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n248\t                }\n249\t            }\n250\t        } else {\n251\t            tracing::debug!(\"Split trigger disabled (threshold = 0)\");\n252\t        }\n253\t\n254\t        // 6. Return result.\n255\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n256\t            tracing::debug!(\"No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\");\n257\t            StrandResult::NoWork\n258\t        } else {\n259\t            let candidate_ids: Vec<&str> = candidates.iter()\n260\t                .map(|b| b.id.as_ref())\n261\t                .collect();\n262\t            tracing::info!(\n263\t                count = candidates.len(),\n264\t                candidates = ?candidate_ids,\n265\t                \"Returning {} candidates for processing\",\n266\t                candidates.len()\n267\t            );\n268\t            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n269\t        }\n270\t    }\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t// ─── Unit tests  }\n561\t\n562\t    fn make_bead_with_labels(id: &str, priority: u8, labels: Vec<&str>) -> Bead {\n563\t        let mut bead = make_bead(id, priority, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\");\n564\t // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n577\t    // Sorting\n578\t    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n579\t\n580\t    #[tokio::test]\n581\t    async fn candidates_sorted_by_priority_then_created_at() {\n582\t        let store = MemoryStore {\n583\t            beads: vec![\n584\t #[tokio::test]\n603\t    async fn tie_broken_by_bead_id() {\n604\t        // Same priority, same created_at — tie broken by id (lexicographic).\n605\t        let store = MemoryStore {\n606\t            beads: vec![\n607\t                make_bead(\"bbb\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n608\t                make_bead(\"aaa\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n609\t                make_bead(\"ccc\", 1, ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n626\t    // Filtering\n627\t    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n628\t\n629\t    #[tokio::test]\n630\t    async fn beads_with_excluded_labels_are_filtered() {\n631\t        let store = MemoryStore {\n632\t            beads: vec![\n633\t                make_bead_with_labels(\"deferred-bead\", 1, vec![\"deferred\"]),\n634\t                make_bead_with_labels(\"human-bead\", 1, vec![\"human\"]),\n635\t }\n916\t}\n917\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"64460ace-4550-4dcd-b6c1-f600c922b352","uuid":"4c3f9a05-c94f-4eee-84c3-0298df5d57ab","timestamp":"2026-07-09T04:38:33.258Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs","content":"//! Pluck strand: primary bead selection from the assigned workspace.\n//!\n//! Pluck handles >90% of all bead processing. It queries the bead store for\n//! unassigned, ready beads, filters by excluded labels, and sorts them in\n//! deterministic priority order: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n//!\n//! Given the same queue state, every worker computes the same candidate list.\n\nuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, Filters};\nuse crate::types::{Bead, StrandError, StrandResult};\n\n/// Default labels excluded from Pluck selection when not configured.\nconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n\n/// The Pluck strand — primary work selection.\npub struct PluckStrand {\n    /// Labels to exclude from candidate selection.\n    exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n    /// Auto-split beads after this many consecutive failures (0 = disabled).\n    split_after_failures: u32,\n}\n\nimpl PluckStrand {\n    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels.\n    ///\n    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n    /// `blocked`) is used.\n    pub fn new(exclude_labels: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n                .iter()\n                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n                .collect()\n        } else {\n            exclude_labels\n        };\n        PluckStrand {\n            exclude_labels: labels,\n            split_after_failures: 3, // default threshold\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels and split threshold.\n    ///\n    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n    /// `blocked`) is used.\n    pub fn with_split_threshold(exclude_labels: Vec<String>, split_after_failures: u32) -> Self {\n        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n                .iter()\n                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n                .collect()\n        } else {\n            exclude_labels\n        };\n        PluckStrand {\n            exclude_labels: labels,\n            split_after_failures,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Extract the failure count from a bead's labels.\n    ///\n    /// Labels follow the pattern `failure-count:N`. Returns the count if found,\n    /// or 0 if no failure-count label exists.\n    fn extract_failure_count(bead: &Bead) -> u32 {\n        bead.labels\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n            .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())\n            .max()\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Sort candidates in deterministic priority order.\n    ///\n    /// Sort key: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n    /// The id tie-breaker ensures identical ordering across platforms.\n    fn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n            a.priority\n                .cmp(&b.priority)\n                .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n        });\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl super::Strand for PluckStrand {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"pluck\"\n    }\n\n    #[tracing::instrument(\n        name = \"strand.pluck\",\n        skip(self, store),\n        fields(\n            strand = \"pluck\",\n            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n            split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n        )\n    )]\n    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n        tracing::debug!(\n            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n            split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n            \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\"\n        );\n\n        // 1. Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n        let filters = Filters {\n            assignee: None,\n            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n        };\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            filters = ?filters,\n            \"Querying bead store for ready candidates\"\n        );\n\n        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n            Ok(beads) => {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    count = beads.len(),\n                    \"Bead store returned {} candidates\",\n                    beads.len()\n                );\n                beads\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"Bead store query failed\"\n                );\n                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n            }\n        };\n\n        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n        let before_label_filter = candidates.len();\n        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n        let after_label_filter = candidates.len();\n\n        if before_label_filter != after_label_filter {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                excluded_count = before_label_filter - after_label_filter,\n                remaining = after_label_filter,\n                excluded_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n                \"Label filtering excluded {} beads\",\n                before_label_filter - after_label_filter\n            );\n\n            // Log each excluded bead at DEBUG level\n            let excluded_beads: Vec<_> = candidates.iter()\n                .filter(|b| b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)))\n                .map(|b| (b.id.as_ref(), b.labels.clone()))\n                .collect();\n            for (id, labels) in excluded_beads {\n                let excluded_reasons: Vec<_> = labels.iter()\n                    .filter(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l))\n                    .map(|l| l.as_str())\n                    .collect();\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %id,\n                    labels = ?labels,\n                    excluded_reasons = ?excluded_reasons,\n                    \"Excluded bead due to labels\"\n                );\n            }\n\n            // Re-filter since we logged them\n            candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                count = after_label_filter,\n                \"No beads excluded by label filter\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker)\n        //    and Open beads with a stale assignee. These are never claimable — the claimer\n        //    will reject them every time, causing a hot loop.\n        let before_status_filter = candidates.len();\n        candidates.retain(|b| {\n            !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n                || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n        });\n        let after_status_filter = candidates.len();\n\n        if before_status_filter != after_status_filter {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                filtered_count = before_status_filter - after_status_filter,\n                remaining = after_status_filter,\n                \"Status/assignee filtering removed {} beads\",\n                before_status_filter - after_status_filter\n            );\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                count = after_status_filter,\n                \"No beads excluded by status/assignee filter\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // 4. Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n        if !candidates.is_empty() {\n            let first = &candidates[0];\n            tracing::debug!(\n                total = candidates.len(),\n                first_bead_id = %first.id,\n                first_priority = first.priority,\n                first_created_at = %first.created_at,\n                \"Sorting {} candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\",\n                candidates.len()\n            );\n        }\n        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n\n        // 5. Check for split trigger: if the first candidate has accumulated\n        //    enough consecutive failures, dispatch a SPLIT instruction instead\n        //    of returning the bead for normal processing.\n        if self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n            if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n                let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n                    failure_count = failure_count,\n                    threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n                    split_triggered = failure_count >= self.split_after_failures,\n                    \"Checking split trigger for first candidate\"\n                );\n\n                if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n                        failure_count = failure_count,\n                        threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n                        \"Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\"\n                    );\n                    return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\"Split trigger disabled (threshold = 0)\");\n        }\n\n        // 6. Return result.\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            tracing::debug!(\"No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\");\n            StrandResult::NoWork\n        } else {\n            let candidate_ids: Vec<&str> = candidates.iter()\n                .map(|b| b.id.as_ref())\n                .collect();\n            tracing::info!(\n                count = candidates.len(),\n                candidates = ?candidate_ids,\n                \"Returning {} candidates for processing\",\n                candidates.len()\n            );\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::bead_store::RepairReport;\n    use crate::types::{BeadId, BeadStatus, ClaimResult};\n\n    use anyhow::Result;\n    use chrono::{TimeZone, Utc};\n    use std::path::PathBuf;\n\n    /// In-memory bead store for testing.\n    struct MemoryStore {\n        beads: Vec<Bead>,\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for MemoryStore {\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(self.beads.clone())\n        }\n        async fn ready(&self, filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            let result: Vec<Bead> = self\n                .beads\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|b| {\n                    // Filter by assignee if set.\n                    if let Some(ref a) = filters.assignee {\n                        if b.assignee.as_ref() != Some(a) {\n                            return false;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Filter out beads with excluded labels.\n                    if b.labels.iter().any(|l| filters.exclude_labels.contains(l)) {\n                        return false;\n                    }\n                    true\n                })\n                .cloned()\n                .collect();\n            Ok(result)\n        }\n\n        async fn show(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            self.beads\n                .iter()\n                .find(|b| b.id == *id)\n                .cloned()\n                .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!(\"bead not found: {id}\"))\n        }\n\n        async fn claim(&self, _id: &BeadId, _actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented\")\n        }\n\n        async fn release(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn flush(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn reopen(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn labels(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n            let bead = self.show(id).await?;\n            Ok(bead.labels)\n        }\n\n        async fn add_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn remove_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn create_bead(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n            Ok(BeadId::from(\"new-bead\".to_string()))\n        }\n\n        async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n NoWork causes the worker\n        // to move to Exhausted rather than spinning the claim-retry loop.\n        let store = UnfilteredStore {\n            beads: vec![\n handlers run in a separate context and\n/// cannot easily access the Worker's shutdown flag directly.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nstatic GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);\n\n/// Set the AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n}\n\n/// Clear the global shutdown flag pointer.\n/// Called when the worker is dropped to avoid dangling pointers.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n    LAST_SIGNAL.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n}\n\n/// Track the last received signal for diagnostic logging.\n/// AtomicU32 allows At least one must be set.\n/// Hooks are fire-and-forget — failures are logged but never block the worker.\n--\n/// OTLP sink configuration for OpenTelemetry export.\n///\n/// When enabled, NEEDLE OtlpSinkConfig {\n    /// Enable or disable the OTLP sink.\n    #[serde(default = \"OtlpSinkConfig::default_enabled\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n\n    /// OTLP endpoint URL (e.g., `http://localhost:4317` for gRPC).\n    #[serde(default = \"OtlpSinkConfig::default_endpoint\")]\n    pub endpoint: String,\n\n    /// Protocol: \"grpc\" or \"http\".\n    #[serde(default = \"OtlpSinkConfig::default_protocol\")]\n    pub protocol: String,\n\n    /// Request timeout in seconds (default: 10).\n    #[serde(default = \"OtlpSinkConfig::default_timeout_secs\")]\n    pub timeout_secs: u64,\n\n    /// Compression: \"gzip\", \"none\", or \"zstd\".\n    #[serde(default = \"OtlpSinkConfig::default_compression\")]\n    pub compression: String,\n\n    /// TLS configuration: \"none\", \"tls\", or \"mtls\".\n    #[serde(default = \"OtlpSinkConfig::default_tls\")]\n    pub tls: String,\n\n    /// HTTP headers to send with each request (format: \"key: value\").\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub headers: Vec<String>,\n\n--\n/// Telemetry configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TelemetryConfig {\n    #[serde(default)]\n than this threshold are considered stale, triggering\n    /// worker failure detection.
